evidence.source_title: The Secret History of Hezbollah — Foundation for Defense of Democracies
evidence.description: Chamran trained in Lebanon with the PLO and later joined the Lebanese Shia resistance that produced Amal and Hezbollah.
Chamran established Shia fighter training infrastructure in southern Lebanon. His protégé Husayn al-Musawi later led the Islamic Amal split that merged into Hezbollah.

editorial_notes: Precursor relationship. Chamran died in June 1981, before Hezbollah was formally founded in 1982. His training camps, fighter networks, and organizational methods in Lebanon were foundational to Hezbollah's creation.
